name: Void-Spark
components: V, S
description: You summon a mote of pure void from the space between worlds. It streaks toward a target, dealing 1d4 necrotic damage and leaving a chilling absence of light. The light within 5 feet of the target is dimmed until the start of your next turn.
higher_levels: At 5th level, the damage increases by 1d4. At 11th level, it increases by another 1d4 (total 3d4). At 17th level, it increases by another 1d4 (total 4d4).

name: Blood-Sense
components: V, S
description: You taste the blood in the air, sensing the location of any creature with blood within 30 feet that is not behind total cover. You instinctively know whether the blood is pure (dragon-blood) or tainted, and if the creature is currently bleeding. Hissing voids and Cinderblessed give no reading.

name: Dragon-Whisper
components: V
description: You attune to the residual essence of the Sovereign Dragons, hearing a faint whisper of ancient draconic knowledge. You gain advantage on your next Intelligence check regarding dragons, the Blood, or the history of Ourobora. The whisper may come as a cryptic phrase or a vision of ash and scale.

name: Aether-Touch
components: S
description: Your hand glows with raw aether energy, letting you gently push or pull unattended objects weighing up to 5 pounds within 30 feet. You can also cause a faint shimmer in the air to distract a creature, giving it disadvantage on the next perception check it makes before the end of its next turn.

name: Forge-Heat
components: V, S
description: You heat a small metal object (like a coin, blade, or chain) to glowing red-hot for a moment. If you touch a creature with the object, it takes 1 fire damage. Otherwise, the heat can ignite flammable materials (paper, cloth) that come into contact with it.

name: Storm-Snap
components: S
description: You snap your fingers, releasing a small bolt of lightning that arcs to a creature within 30 feet, dealing 1d4 lightning damage. The crack of thunder is audible up to 100 feet away. If the target is wearing metal armor, you have advantage on the attack roll.
higher_levels: At 5th level, the damage increases by 1d4. At 11th level, it increases by another 1d4 (total 3d4). At 17th level, it increases by another 1d4 (total 4d4).

name: Flesh-Knit
components: V, S
description: You weave flesh together with aether, closing a superficial wound. You restore 1 hit point to a living creature you touch. This cannot stabilize a dying creature or repair major injuries, but it stops minor bleeding and gives a faint scar.

name: Bone-Rattle
components: V, S
description: You cause the bones of a creature within 60 feet to shudder and ache. The target must make a Constitution saving throw or take 1d4 necrotic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn. The feeling is akin to a deep, jarring chill.
higher_levels: At 5th level, the damage increases by 1d4. At 11th level, it increases by another 1d4 (total 3d4). At 17th level, it increases by another 1d4 (total 4d4).

name: Ash-Cloud
components: V, S, M (a pinch of ash)
description: You conjure a small cloud of ash and soot in a 5-foot cube within range. It lightly obscures the area and causes a choking sensation. Creatures that breathe in the area must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be unable to speak for 1 round. The cloud is dissipated by a moderate wind.

name: Crystal-Glint
components: S
description: You cause a Blood Crystal or other crystalline object to gleam with a faint inner light. For the duration, you can see through the crystal as if it were a window, perceiving the faint aetheric patterns of the area. This reveals invisible aetheric residues left by magic cast within the last minute, but not creatures.

name: Sump-Mist
components: V, S
description: You exhale a mist of toxic fumes reminiscent of the Sump. The mist fills a 10-foot cube within range, lightly obscuring it for the duration. Creatures that start their turn in the mist must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of their next turn.

name: Iron-Rust
components: V, S
description: You cause a metal object you touch to rapidly corrode. A nonmagical metal object up to 1 foot in diameter crumbles to rust. If the object is worn or carried, the creature holding it must make a Dexterity saving throw or the object is ruined. Precious metals like gold and silver are immune.

name: Obsidian-Shard
components: S, M (a small piece of obsidian)
description: You shape a shard of obsidian from the ground or from a piece of rock and hurl it at a target. Make a ranged spell attack; on a hit, it deals 1d8 piercing damage. The shard shatters on impact, leaving sharp fragments that deal 1 slashing damage to any creature within 5 feet of the target.
higher_levels: At 5th level, the damage increases by 1d8. At 11th level, it increases by another 1d8 (total 3d8). At 17th level, it increases by another 1d8 (total 4d8).

name: Cult-Chant
components: V
description: You intone a low, resonant chant that unsettles your enemies. All creatures of your choice within 30 feet that can hear you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn. The chant is obviously magical and draws attention.

name: Blanks-Resolve
components: V, S
description: A flicker of defiant will empowers you against purity-based magic. For the duration, you gain advantage on saving throws against spells and effects that rely on blood purity or dragon-origin (such as many Solis-Grave spells). Additionally, you can sense the purity level of any creature within 30 feet as if using the Blood-Sense cantrip.

name: Bloodlight
components: S
description: You touch a surface and leave a glowing crimson mark that sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius. The mark is only visible to creatures with blood. It lasts for 1 hour or until dismissed.

name: Inquisitor's Eye
components: V, S
description: You focus on a creature you can see within 30 feet and mentally probe its blood. You learn its blood purity as a rough percentage (within 5%). If the creature's purity is above 50%, you take 1 psychic damage from the recoil of sensing high power.

name: Void Whisper
components: V
description: You listen to the void between sounds. For the duration, you can understand the literal meaning of any spoken language you hear, but you also hear faint whispers of the dead in the background that can be distracting.

name: Wilt
components: S
description: You cause a non-magical plant within 10 feet to instantly wither and crumble to dust. This can destroy crops, erase evidence, or create a small area of barren ground.

name: Ash Step
components: S, M (a pinch of ash)
description: For the duration, your footsteps leave no tracks and instead leave a thin layer of ash that obscures your path. You cannot be tracked by mundane means, and creatures using scent to track you have disadvantage.

name: Skullflame
components: V, S, M (a skull or large bone)
description: You ignite a cold, blue flame on the brow of a skull or bone you hold. It sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. The flame does not produce heat and cannot ignite objects.

name: Bleeding Edge
components: S, M (a drop of your own blood)
description: You touch a bladed weapon you hold or that is within reach. For 1 minute, the blade is slick with your blood, dealing an extra 1 necrotic damage on the next successful hit. The blood then coagulates and the effect ends.

name: Carrion Scent
components: V, S
description: You enhance your sense of smell to detect decay. For the duration, you can sense the presence of corpses or undead creatures within 60 feet. You know the direction and distance of the nearest source, but not exact numbers.

name: Soul Anchor
casting_time: 1 reaction, which you take when a creature you can see dies
components: V, S
description: As a creature dies within 30 feet of you, you anchor its departing soul for a fleeting moment. You may ask it one question, which it answers in a whisper in a language you understand before fading. The answer is brief and truthful.

name: Bloody Handprint
components: V, S, M (a trace of blood)
description: You create a magically appearing bloody handprint on a surface within range. It is faintly warm and smells of copper. Creatures can determine it is magical with a successful Intelligence (Arcana) check against your spell save DC.

name: Chains of Habit
components: V, S
description: You target a willing creature within 30 feet. For the duration, they feel a mild compulsion to follow a routine they performed within the last day (such as sharpening a blade, pacing a certain path, or lighting a candle). This does not override their free will but makes the action feel natural.

name: Fever Dream
components: V, S
description: You fill a 5-foot cube within range with a swirling miasma of shadows and whispers. Creatures in the area have disadvantage on Perception checks and feel a sense of unease. The miasma is faintly visible as a heat shimmer.

name: Unmake
components: V, S
description: You touch a non-magical object weighing 1 pound or less. The object crumbles into dust. This can destroy locks, fasteners, small tools, or items of evidence. A creature holding the object can make a Dexterity saving throw to keep it intact.

name: Sanguine Sight
components: V, S
description: Your eyes glow faintly red and you perceive the pulsing flow of blood in living creatures within 30 feet. You can see heartbeats as rhythmic flashes and detect if a creature is injured, poisoned, or diseased. You have advantage on Wisdom (Medicine) checks to stabilize a creature.

name: Aether Tracer
components: V, S, M (a drop of blood from the creature to be tracked)
description: You enchant a sample of blood or other bodily fluid from a creature. For the duration, you know the general direction and approximate distance to that creature, as long as it is on the same plane. The link breaks if the creature dies or the sample is destroyed.

name: Blood Lance
components: S, M (a drop of the caster's blood)
description: A lance of congealed blood erupts from your palm and streaks toward a creature you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 piercing damage. You regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt, rounded down.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Void Grasp
components: V, S, M (a fragment of obsidian)
description: Shadowy tendrils from the Void erupt from the ground in a 10-foot square centered on a point you choose within range. Each creature in that area must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained for the duration. A restrained creature can use its action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC, ending the effect on itself on a success.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the area increases by 5 feet for each slot level above 1st.

name: Aether Bolt
components: V, S, M (a shard of aether-cracked crystal)
description: You hurl a bolt of raw aether energy at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 force damage. If the target is a construct or undead, it takes an additional 1d8 force damage.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st (the bonus against constructs/undead increases by 1d8 at 3rd and 5th level).

name: Cinder Whip
components: V, S
description: A whip of smoldering dragonfire lances from your hand toward a creature you can see within range. Make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 fire damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 10 feet toward you.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the fire damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Chains of Penitence
components: V, S, M (a broken chain link from a cathedral)
description: Spectral iron chains spring forth from the ground and wrap around a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be restrained for the duration. While restrained, the creature takes 1d4 radiant damage at the start of each of its turns. A restrained creature can use its action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC, ending the effect on a success.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the radiant damage increases by 1d4 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Flesh Mend
components: V, S, M (a bandage soaked in blood)
description: You channel necrotic energy to knit flesh and sinew. A creature you touch regains hit points equal to 1d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier. If the creature is a construct or undead, it regains no hit points. Instead, the spell fails, and you must make a Spell Safety saving throw (DC equal to your spell save DC) or suffer 1d4 necrotic damage.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the healing increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Desecrate
components: V, S, M (a handful of grave dirt)
description: You corrupt a 20-foot-radius sphere of ground centered on a point you choose within range. The area becomes difficult terrain for creatures of the Church or of good alignment. Undead creatures in the area have advantage on attack rolls and saving throws. Any creature that dies in the area must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or have its spirit bound to the location, preventing resurrection unless the caster allows it.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the radius increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 1st.

name: Scales of Judgment
components: V, S
description: Spectral scales of justice form around you. For the duration, you gain a +2 bonus to AC and resistance to radiant damage. Whenever a creature within 30 feet of you receives healing, you may reduce that healing by half (rounded down) as a reaction, transferring the absorbed energy to yourself as temporary hit points equal to the amount reduced.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the AC bonus increases by 1 for each two slot levels above 1st.

name: Whisper of the Skull
components: V, S, M (a humanoid skull)
description: You place your hand upon a skull and command the spirit within to answer one question. The skull speaks a short phrase or shows a vision. The answer is limited to what the creature knew in life. If the skull has been cleaned or tampered with, the answer may be corrupted or silent. This spell cannot contact a soul that has been resurrected or destroyed.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you may ask one additional question for each slot level above 1st, up to 5 questions total.

name: Sanguine Shield
components: V, S, M (a drop of the caster's blood)
description: Blood seeps from your skin and coagulates into a shimmering crimson barrier. You gain a +2 bonus to AC and resistance to necrotic damage. The shield is visible and counts as a light source (dim light in a 5-foot radius). At the end of each of your turns, if you have taken damage since your last turn, the shield steals 1 hit point from you (this damage cannot be reduced).
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the AC bonus increases by 1 for each slot level above 1st (max +5).

name: Void Step
components: V
description: You step through the Void, instantly teleporting to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet. A trail of shadowy afterimages marks your passage. Until the start of your next turn, you have advantage on the first attack roll you make against a creature that was within 5 feet of your starting position.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the teleportation distance increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 1st.

name: Burning Brand
components: V, S, M (a holy symbol of the Church)
description: You touch a creature and sear a holy symbol into its flesh. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 2d6 fire damage and be branded for the duration. The brand sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius and glows brightly when within 30 feet of a desecrated object or creature. While branded, the target cannot hide from you and you have advantage on Perception checks to locate it.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the fire damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Aether Sight
components: V, S, M (a tiny prism)
description: Your eyes shimmer with aetherial light. For the duration, you can see invisible creatures and objects, and you gain the ability to perceive aetheric residue and magical trails. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks to detect hidden or disguised magical effects. The spell does not allow you to see through solid objects.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the duration increases by 1 hour for each slot level above 1st (max 4 hours).

name: Corrupting Touch
components: V, S
description: Your hand becomes wreathed in dark, rotting energy. Make a melee spell attack against a creature you touch. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 necrotic damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have its hit point maximum reduced by the damage dealt. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest or receives a greater restoration spell.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the necrotic damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st, and the hit point maximum reduction increases by an equal amount.

name: Rite of Binding
components: V, S, M (a iron collar worth 10 gp)
description: You attempt to bind the will of a creature with low blood purity. Choose a humanoid you can see within range. If the target's blood purity percentage is 14% or lower, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for the duration. While charmed, the target obeys your verbal commands to the best of its ability, but will not obey commands that are obviously suicidal or directly harmful to itself. If the target's blood purity is 0%, it has disadvantage on the saving throw.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st, provided each target has blood purity 14% or lower.

name: Sanguine Mend
components: V, S, M (a drop of the caster's own blood)
description: You press a bleeding hand to the wound of a creature you touch. The target's blood surges, knitting flesh with a feverish warmth. They regain 1d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier hit points, but take 1 point of necrotic damage as the cost of stolen vitality.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the healing increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st, and the self-damage increases by 1 per die.
classes: Cleric, Ichor Sorcerer, Blood Domain Cleric

name: Aether Ward
components: V, S, M (a shard of obsidian)
description: You trace a rune of protection on the target's skin. A shimmering film of raw aether envelops them. Until the spell ends, the target gains a +2 bonus to AC and has resistance to force damage. Each time they are hit by a melee attack, the ward flashes with crimson light and you can choose to end the spell early to deal 1d4 force damage to the attacker.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the bonus to AC increases by +1 for every two slot levels above 1st.

name: Corrupting Touch
components: V, S
description: Your hand crackles with void-tainted energy. Make a melee spell attack against a creature. On a hit, the target's flesh withers and blackens, taking 2d8 necrotic damage. If the target is a living creature (not undead or construct), it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of its next turn as the corruption seeps into its blood.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Void Echo
components: V, S
description: You whisper a fragment of the Betrayed's name into the aether, and the sound rebounds inside the target's mind. Choose one creature within range. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be haunted by phantom whispers, suffering disadvantage on its next attack roll before the end of its next turn. While the spell lasts, the target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.

name: Brand of Submission
components: V, S, M (a small iron brand heated in dragonfire)
description: You sear the mark of obedience into the target's flesh. Choose one creature you can see within range. It must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it is unable to willingly move closer to you and has disadvantage on attack rolls against you. On a success, the spell ends. The brand glows with a dull red heat for the duration, dealing 1 fire damage to the target at the start of each of its turns.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage per turn increases by 1 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Cinder Skin
components: V, S, M (a handful of ash from a defeated enemy)
description: You coat the target's skin with a layer of smoldering embers, granting them resistance to fire damage. Additionally, any creature that hits them with a melee attack takes 1d4 fire damage. The embers shed dim light in a 5-foot radius.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the melee retaliation damage increases by 1d4 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Fleshbind
components: V, S, M (a strip of dried sinew)
description: You force torn flesh to reknit with a painful snap. The target regains 1d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier hit points, but must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn from the agony of reconnecting nerves.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the healing increases by 1d8 per slot level above 1st, and the incapacitation save DC increases by 1.

name: Oath of Iron
components: V, S
description: You recite a binding oath from the Iron Codex, and your skin takes on a metallic sheen. Until the spell ends, you gain a +2 bonus to AC against attacks from creatures that have dealt damage to you within the last minute. Additionally, you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the AC bonus increases by +1 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Shadow Leech
components: V, S, M (a leech preserved in brine)
description: You summon a wisp of shadow that latches onto a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 1d6 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns, and you regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. The target can use its action to tear the shadow off, ending the spell, but takes 1d6 necrotic damage as it does so.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the initial and subsequent damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Blighted Breath
components: V, S, M (a rotten tooth)
description: You exhale a cloud of greenish, foul-smelling vapor that reeks of decay. Each creature in a 15-foot cone must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 2d6 poison damage and is poisoned for 1 minute. On a success, it takes half damage and is not poisoned. A poisoned creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Ward of Skulls
components: V, S, M (a miniature skull carved from bone)
description: You bless a door, window, or other entryway with the warding of the Five Skulls. For the duration, the first creature that attempts to open or pass through the warded portal without your permission must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it takes 2d8 psychic damage and is frightened of the portal for 1 minute. On a success, it takes half damage and is not frightened. The ward glows with a faint malevolent light.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the psychic damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Bloodletting
components: V, S
description: You cause a wound on a creature you can see within range to hemorrhage profusely. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 1d4 slashing damage at the start of each of its turns as blood seeps from its body. Additionally, any critical hit against the target while the spell is active deals an extra 1d6 damage of the attack's type. The target can use its action to staunch the bleeding, ending the spell.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the initial and recurring damage increases by 1d4 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Aether Siphon
components: V, S, M (a hollow glass tube)
description: You drain ambient aether from a living creature to restore your own vitality. Choose one creature within range. It takes 2d6 necrotic damage. You then regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. If this healing exceeds your hit point maximum, you gain temporary hit points equal to the excess.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Grave Dampen
casting_time: 1 reaction, which you take when you or a creature within 30 feet is subjected to a necrotic damage effect
components: V
description: You intone a prayer to the Five Skulls, dulling the hunger of necrotic energy. Choose one creature within range (including yourself). The necrotic damage it takes from the triggering effect is reduced by 2d6 + your spellcasting ability modifier. At higher levels, the reduction increases.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the reduction increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Purge the Weak
components: V, S, M (a broken chain link)
description: You fill a creature with the certainty that it is unworthy. Choose one creature within range. It must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks for the duration. Additionally, if it tries to take the Attack action, it must first succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or instead use its action to do nothing, paralyzed by self-doubt. The target can repeat the Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on a success.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.
classes: Cleric (Inquisition Domain), Warlock (Archfey, reskinned as Archfiend of Doubt)

name: Aether Lance
components: V, S, M (a shard of obsidian)
description: You thrust your hand forward, unleashing a lance of concentrated aether. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 3d8 force damage. If the target is a creature with blood purity less than 40%, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be pushed 10 feet away and knocked prone.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 2nd.

name: Brand of Heresy
components: V, S, M (a pinch of ash from a burned heretic)
description: You trace a sigil of the Church on a creature you touch. For the duration, the target is branded with a visible mark of shame. It has disadvantage on Charisma checks and saving throws against being frightened. Additionally, any radiant or fire damage dealt to the target ignores resistance. The brand can be removed by a Remove Curse spell or by the touch of a high-ranking Inquisitor.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the duration increases by 1 minute for each slot level above 2nd.

name: Crimson Offerings
components: V, S, M (a drop of your own blood)
description: You expend one Hit Die and roll it. You regain hit points equal to the roll + your spellcasting ability modifier. Then, each ally within 30 feet that is below half their maximum hit points gains temporary hit points equal to half the amount you healed (minimum 1). Blood-crazed creatures (such as those under the effects of a blood frenzy) instead take that amount as necrotic damage.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you may expend an additional Hit Die for each slot level above 2nd, adding its roll to the healing.

name: Dragon’s Ascent
components: V, S, M (a scale from a dragon)
description: You touch a willing creature. For the duration, the creature gains a flying speed of 40 feet. If it ends its turn in the air without moving, it descends 20 feet. Additionally, it can use a bonus action to exhale a burst of fire in a 15-foot cone (DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, 3d6 fire damage on a failure, half on success). Once it uses this breath, it cannot do so again until the spell ends.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the flying speed increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 2nd.

name: Echoes of the First Sin
components: V, S, M (a bloodstained parchment of the Betrayal)
description: You attune to the lingering trauma of the God-Killer’s act. For the duration, you gain a spectral vision of the past. You can see and hear events that occurred in a 30-foot radius around you up to 1,000 years ago, but only if they involve violent death, betrayal, or bloodshed. The visions are shadowy and fragmented. A successful Insight check (DC 15) may reveal hidden details. The spell leaves you vulnerable; you have disadvantage on Perception checks against hidden creatures while maintaining concentration.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can see events up to an additional 500 years into the past for each slot level above 2nd.

name: Flesh-Shaper’s Grasp
components: V, S, M (a strand of sinew)
description: Choose a creature you can see within range. It must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have its flesh warp and twist. On a failure, the target’s speed is halved, it cannot take reactions, and it has disadvantage on Strength checks and saving throws. At the start of each of your turns, the target may repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. If the target’s blood purity is 30% or less, it also takes 1d6 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns while affected.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the necrotic damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 2nd.

name: Gaze of the One-Eyed God
components: V, S, M (a small obsidian mirror)
description: You open a third eye on your forehead, seeing the hidden truths in a 30-foot cone. All illusions and magical disguises within the area are revealed as such, and invisible creatures and objects become visible to you until the end of your next turn. Additionally, you learn the blood purity of each creature (if any) as a numerical percentage. Creatures with a purity of 80% or higher are outlined in a golden aura, while blanks (0%) appear as cold shadows.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the cone’s range increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 2nd.

name: Hex of Unsleeping

While affected, it has disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened or stunned. The spell ends if the target receives a Remove Curse or if it is rendered unconscious by any means (which has no effect on the exhaustion).
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the duration increases by 1 hour for each slot level above 2nd.

If the target has a blood purity of 10% or less (blank or common), it takes 1d6 radiant damage each time it tries to escape. The shackles can be dispelled (DC 16) or broken by dealing 30 damage to them (AC 18, immune to nonmagical bludgeoning and slashing).

If the target’s blood purity is less than 50%, it also takes an extra 1d8 necrotic damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of your next turn.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the base damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 2nd.

higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the fire damage on a melee hit increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 2nd.

The spell leaves a scorched mark on the ground that glows faintly for 1 minute.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the fire damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 2nd.

At the end of each of its turns, the target may repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the psychic damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 2nd.

components: V, S
description: You reach into the soul of a creature you can see within range and tear a fragment loose. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it takes 6d6 necrotic damage, and you regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. Additionally, the target has disadvantage on the next death saving throw it makes within the next minute (if applicable). If the target is reduced to 0 hit points by this spell, its soul is destroyed, making it impossible to resurrect via Raise Dead or similar magic unless a true resurrection or wish spell is used.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 3rd.

name: Cinder Storm
components: V, S, M (a handful of ash from a cremated dragon-worshipper)
description: A swirling storm of burning cinders and hot ash fills a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you choose within range. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes 5d6 fire damage and is Blinded until the end of its next turn. On a success, it takes half damage and is not Blinded. The area becomes heavily obscured by ash and smoke until the end of your next turn. The cinders cling to surfaces, leaving scorch marks that cannot be easily cleaned.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the fire damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3rd.

name: Grave Command
components: V, S, M (a handful of dirt from a grave)
description: You touch a corpse that has been dead for no longer than 1 hour and force it to speak a single phrase of up to 25 words as it did in life. The corpse can only answer a question you ask, using knowledge it possessed while alive. It must be physically capable of speech (jaw intact, etc.). The answer is spoken in its natural voice, often with a hollow, rasping quality. This spell does not animate the corpse; it merely compels it to speak. Once used, the corpse cannot be targeted by this spell again. The spell fails if the corpse's soul is trapped elsewhere or destroyed.

name: Purity Bolt
components: V, S, M (a drop of holy water blessed by an Inquisitor)
description: You fire a bolt of pure, searing light that represents the Church's judgment. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 6d10 radiant damage. If the target has a blood purity of 14% or lower (Common or Blank), it instead takes 8d10 radiant damage and is Stunned until the end of its next turn. This spell is especially effective against creatures of impure blood or heretics. The bolt leaves a lingering, faintly glowing wound that sheds dim light for 1 minute.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 3rd.

name: Shadow of the Sovereign
components: V, S, M (a scale from a Sovereign Dragon, or a facsimile blessed by the Church)
description: You evoke a terrifying, shadowy image of one of the Six Sovereign Dragons (your choice) in a 20-foot-cube within range. Each creature in that area that can see the illusion must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it takes 4d8 psychic damage and is Frightened of the illusion for the duration. On a success, it takes half damage and is not Frightened. A Frightened creature must use its action each turn to Dash away from the illusion and can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. The illusion is semi-transparent and has a physical presence that can block line of sight but cannot be harmed. If you have at least 70% blood purity, the illusion can also roar once as a bonus action, forcing all creatures within 30 feet that can hear to make a Wisdom save or be Frightened for 1 round.

name: Crimson Chains
components: V, S, M (a length of chain soaked in blood)
description: Spectral chains made of congealed blood erupt from the ground or a surface within range, attempting to bind one creature you can see. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it is Restrained by the chains for the duration. A Restrained creature takes 2d6 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns as the chains drain its blood. The creature can use its action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, breaking free on a success. The chains are visible but not solid to other creatures, who can move through the target's space normally.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage per turn increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3rd.

name: Corrupting Aether
components: V, S, M (a small vial of aetherite dust mixed with powdered black onyx)
description: You imbue the ambient aether in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on yourself with corrupting energy. For the duration, the area becomes dimly lit with a sickly purple glow. Any creature (other than you) that starts its turn in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 2d6 necrotic damage and its maximum hit points are reduced by the same amount until the end of its next turn. Constructs and undead are immune. Additionally, any spell of 1st level or higher cast by a creature hostile to you in the area has its spell slot level reduced by 1 (minimum 1st) for the purposes of all effects, as the aether is tainted. This effect does not stack with itself.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the necrotic damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3rd.

name: Ritual of the Five Skulls
components: V, S, M (five skulls of sentient beings, each from a different race, arranged in a pentagram)
description: You perform a profane ritual over the body of a creature that died within the last 24 hours. The skulls glow with malevolent light as you force the corpse to rise as a zombie under your control. The zombie is created as per the Animate Dead spell, but with a few differences: it has maximum hit points equal to 2d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier + half your level; it deals an extra 1d6 necrotic damage on its attacks; and it radiates an aura of dread, causing hostile creatures within 10 feet to have disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened. You may have up to three such zombies under your control at a time. If you attempt to create a fourth, the oldest one crumbles to dust. This spell is considered a forbidden practice by the Church of the Five Skulls and using it in public may result in excommunication or worse.

name: Aether Overload
components: V, S, M (a fragment of a Blood Crystal worth 100 gp)
description: You unleash a devastating pulse of raw aether from your core. Each creature of your choice within range must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 8d6 force damage and gain one level of exhaustion. On a success, they take half damage and no exhaustion. This damage ignores resistance and immunity to force damage.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Bloodfire Lance
components: V, S, M (a drop of the caster's blood)
description: A lance of searing bloodfire erupts from your palm and streaks toward a creature you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 6d6 fire damage and 6d6 necrotic damage. The fire damage ignores resistance and immunity to fire. The necrotic damage reduces the target's hit point maximum by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, each damage die increases by 1d6 (e.g., 7d6 fire and 7d6 necrotic at 5th level).

name: Void Bolt
components: V, S
description: You hurl a bolt of crystallized void energy at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 8d8 cold damage and must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished to a harmless demiplane for 1 minute. The banishment ends if the target takes any damage while banished. If the target is native to another plane, the banishment lasts for 1 minute regardless of damage.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the cold damage increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Dragon's Wrath
components: V, S
description: You exhale a torrent of dragonfire infused with the essence of the Five Skulls. Each creature in a 60-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 9d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. Creatures that fail the save also catch fire, taking 2d6 fire damage at the start of each of their turns until they or an ally uses an action to extinguish the flames.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the initial damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Soul Rend
components: V, S
description: You tear at the very soul of a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 8d6 necrotic damage and their hit point maximum is reduced by the same amount. You regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this spell dies instantly, its soul consumed.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Crown of Thorns
components: V, S, M (a circlet of twisted iron)
description: You force a cursed crown of psychic thorns onto the mind of a creature you can see. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it takes 6d8 psychic damage and gains a short-term madness effect (roll on the Short-Term Madness table). For the duration, at the start of each of its turns, it takes 1d8 psychic damage and must repeat the saving throw or the madness persists and the damage continues. On a success, the creature takes half damage and the spell ends.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the initial damage increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Aetherial Chains
components: V, S, M (a length of silver chain worth 50 gp)
description: You summon chains of solid aether that erupt from the ground and wrap around a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it is restrained for the duration. While restrained, the creature takes 2d8 force damage at the start of each of its turns. The restrained creature can use its action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, breaking the chains on a success. If the creature is an archon or sovereignblood, the damage increases to 3d8 and the check is made with disadvantage.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Cremation of Betrayal
components: V, S, M (a pinch of ash from one convicted of treachery)
description: You ignite the blood of a creature you can see, causing their body to combust from within. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 6d10 fire damage. If this damage reduces the target to 20 hit points or fewer, the creature is instantly reduced to a pile of ash (no death saves). A creature killed this way cannot be resurrected by any spell less than true resurrection or wish. On a success, the target takes half damage and is not reduced to ash.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d10 for each slot level above 4th, and the ash threshold increases by 5 (e.g., 25 hit points at 5th level).

name: Bloodstorm
components: V, S, M (a vial of blood from a slain enemy)
description: You call down a storm of crimson blood in a 40-foot-radius cylinder centered on a point you can see within range. The area becomes heavily obscured. Each creature that starts its turn in the storm or enters it for the first time on a turn must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 3d8 necrotic damage and are blinded until the end of their next turn. On a success, they take half damage and are not blinded. The blood also coats the ground, making it difficult terrain.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Mark of the Sovereign
components: V, S, M (a scale from a dragon)
description: You place a visible brand of draconic sigils upon a creature you can see. The mark glows with the color of a dragon’s eye. For the duration, the target cannot benefit from invisibility, and whenever it casts a spell, it takes 2d8 psychic damage. Additionally, you always know the target’s exact location as long as you are on the same plane. If the target is a spellcaster, it has disadvantage on concentration checks while marked.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the psychic damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Infernal Geyser
components: V, S
description: You cause a geyser of molten rock and dragonfire to erupt from the ground at a point you can see within range. Each creature in a 10-foot radius, 30-foot tall cylinder centered on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 6d8 fire damage and 4d8 bludgeoning damage and are pushed 15 feet upward, falling prone upon landing. On a success, they take half damage and are not pushed. The geyser leaves a patch of molten rock that remains for 1 minute, dealing 2d8 fire damage to any creature that ends its turn in that space.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the fire damage increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Shadow of the Flesh-Shaper
components: V, S, M (a scrap of flesh from a corpse)
description: You animate the shadow of a creature you can see within range. The shadow tears itself free from its owner and becomes a hostile, independent creature with the statistics of a shadow (MM) but with 40 hit points and resistance to nonmagical weapons. The shadow acts on your turn, obeying your verbal commands. It can make a Strength drain attack dealing 2d8+3 necrotic damage. If the original creature dies while the shadow is active, the shadow gains 20 temporary hit points. The shadow dissipates when the spell ends or when reduced to 0 hit points.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the shadow's hit points increase by 10 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Murder of Crows
components: V, S, M (a crow's feather dipped in blood)
description: You summon a swarm of aether-infused crows that fill a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within range. The area is heavily obscured. Each creature in the sphere when it appears must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 6d6 piercing damage and 4d6 psychic damage, and are blinded until the end of their next turn. On a success, they take half damage and are not blinded. The crows remain for the duration, dealing 3d6 psychic damage to any creature that ends its turn inside the sphere. Creatures with blood purity 40% or higher take an additional 1d6 psychic damage from the crows' scornful cries.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the initial damage increases by 2d6 piercing and 1d6 psychic for each slot level above 4th.

name: Voidstep
components: V, S
description: You step through a rift in the void, reappearing in an unoccupied space you can see within 60 feet. When you disappear, each creature within 10 feet of your starting space must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 5d10 cold damage and are frightened until the end of their next turn. On a success, they take half damage and are not frightened. When you reappear, each creature within 10 feet of your destination must make a similar saving throw or take 5d10 psychic damage and be stunned until the end of their next turn (half damage, no stun on success).
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage dice each increase by 1d10 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Scales of the Betrayed
components: V, S, M (a dragon scale worth at least 200 gp)
description: You shroud yourself in spectral draconic scales that reflect the colors of the Five Skulls. For the duration, you gain resistance to fire, cold, lightning, and poison damage. Additionally, when a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to deal 3d8 psychic damage to that creature. This damage also reduces the attacker's blood purity by 1% for 24 hours (no effect on creatures with 0% purity). The scales grant you advantage on saving throws against being frightened.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the psychic damage from your reaction increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Blood Boil
components: V, S, M (a drop of the caster's blood)
description: You cause the blood of a creature you can see within range to boil in their veins. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 8d6 fire damage and are poisoned for the duration. On a success, they take half damage and are not poisoned. At the end of each of its turns, the target can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Void Rend
components: V, S, M (a shard of obsidian from the Sulfur Wastes)
description: You tear open a rift to the Void, unleashing a cone of absolute blackness. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 8d8 necrotic damage and are blinded until the end of your next turn. On a success, they take half damage and are not blinded. The rift closes immediately after, leaving a faint smell of ozone and ash.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Aether Whip
components: V, S
description: You conjure a whip of crackling aether energy in your free hand. For the duration, you can make a melee spell attack against a creature within 15 feet. On a hit, the target takes 6d6 lightning damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 10 feet toward you. The whip sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius and can be used to interact with objects or pull levers.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Soul Brand
components: V, S, M (a branding iron heated in dragon fire)
description: You press a searing mark onto a willing or incapacitated creature's flesh. The brand glows with a sickly red light for a moment before scarring into a permanent symbol. For as long as the brand remains, you can sense the creature's location as long as you are on the same plane, and you can use an action to inflict 4d6 psychic damage on it, causing it to relive the branding pain. The brand can be removed only by a Greater Restoration spell or similar magic.

name: Flesh Weave
components: V, S, M (a needle and sinew from a dead creature)
description: You reshape the flesh of a willing or unconscious creature you touch. You can seal wounds (restoring up to 6d8 hit points), close a gaping injury, or even reattach a severed limb if applied within 1 minute of the amputation. Alternatively, you can cause grotesque growths—bone spurs, extra eyes, or nonfunctional limbs—on an unwilling creature, which must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be stunned for 1 minute as its body writhes.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the healing increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Inquisitor's Gaze
components: V, S, M (the eye of a heretic, preserved in brine)
description: Your eyes turn pitch black, and you gain the ability to see the truth. For the duration, you have truesight out to a range of 60 feet. Additionally, you can automatically detect lies spoken by creatures you can see, and you know if a creature is hiding its true intentions (as per the Insight skill with advantage). The Inquisitor's Gaze is painful—you have disadvantage on Perception checks relying on sight due to the strain.

name: Black Candle
components: V, S, M (a candle made of human fat and a pinch of grave dust)
description: You craft a sinister candle that, when lit, allows you to see and communicate with a specific dead creature whose soul has not moved on. While the candle burns (up to 1 hour per spell slot level), you can ask the spirit up to five questions. The spirit answers truthfully to the best of its knowledge, but its memory may be flawed or biased. The candle emits a faint, sickly light and smells of decay. If the candle is extinguished before the hour is up, the spirit departs and cannot be summoned again until the next dawn.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the candle burns for an additional hour per slot level above 4th.

name: Martyr's Sacrifice
casting_time: 1 reaction, which you take when an ally within 60 feet is hit by an attack or fails a saving throw
components: V, S
description: You intercept the harm meant for an ally, taking the damage yourself. The ally takes no damage from the triggering attack or effect, and you take the full damage instead. This damage cannot be reduced or redirected in any way. Additionally, you gain temporary hit points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier (minimum 1) for 1 minute as your devotion shields you from despair.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you gain additional temporary hit points equal to the slot level.

name: Dragon's Descent
components: V, S, M (the scale of a dragon)
description: You call down a column of searing dragon fire from the sky at a point you can see within range. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-tall cylinder must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 10d6 fire damage and are knocked prone. On a success, they take half damage and are not knocked prone. The ground in the area becomes scorched and difficult terrain for 1 minute.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Sever Purity
components: V, S, M (a sliver of Blood Crystal from a condemned heretic)
description: You touch a creature and attempt to drain its dragon blood. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it loses 2d6 of its Blood Purity percentage (if using the percentile system; otherwise, its bloodline is weakened, granting disadvantage on Constitution saves related to magic for 1 hour). A creature reduced to 0% purity becomes a Blank and may die from the shock. This spell is considered a heresy by the Church and is punishable by death.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the purity loss increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Crimson Barrier
components: V, S, M (a vial of the caster's own blood, which is consumed)
description: You draw a circle of your own blood around you, which erupts into a shimmering crimson barrier. The barrier is a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on you. Any creature of your choice that enters the barrier or starts its turn inside it must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it is repelled to the nearest unoccupied space outside the barrier and takes 4d6 psychic damage from the violation. The barrier also grants you half cover (+2 AC) against attacks from outside.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Summon Ashen Thrall
components: V, S, M (a handful of ash from a burned corpse)
description: You call forth an ashen thrall—a shambling creature of soot and bone—in an unoccupied space you can see. The thrall uses the stats of a **Spirit of the Dead** (see Monster Manual, reskinned). It obeys your commands, has a fly speed of 30 feet, and deals necrotic damage. It crumbles to dust when reduced to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The thrall is immune to poison and exhaustion.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you summon one additional thrall for each slot level above 4th.

name: Church's Wrath
components: V, S, M (a holy symbol of the Church of the Five Skulls)
description: You unleash a line of brilliant, searing white fire 60 feet long and 5 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 8d6 radiant damage and are blinded until the end of your next turn. On a success, they take half damage and are not blinded. Undead and fiends have disadvantage on this saving throw.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Cultist's Embrace
components: V, S, M (a drop of ink from a forbidden cult tome)
description: You whisper forbidden truths into the mind of a creature you can see. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it is charmed by you for the duration. While charmed, it regards you as its revered leader and will follow your commands except those that would cause direct harm to itself. Additionally, the target's alignment shifts one step toward evil for the duration. On a success, the target is immune to this spell for 24 hours.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 4th.

name: Blood Crystal Shards
components: V, S, M (a shard of Blood Crystal taken from a willing donor)
description: You summon a storm of razor-sharp Blood Crystal shards that erupt from your body in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 6d10 piercing damage and begin bleeding, losing 1d4 hit points at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute or until a successful DC 15 Medicine check or any magical healing. On a success, they take half damage and do not bleed.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the initial damage increases by 2d10 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Ignivar's Wrath
components: V, S, M (a pinch of ash from a conquered city)
description: You unleash a cataclysmic wave of dragonfire that consumes everything in its path. All creatures of your choice within a 60-foot radius must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 10d6 fire damage and are ignited for 1d4 rounds, taking 2d6 fire damage at the start of each of their turns. On a success, they take half damage and are not ignited. The ground in the area becomes superheated, dealing 1d6 fire damage to any creature that ends its turn there for the next minute. This spell is a favored weapon of the Ignis Inquisition, often used to purge entire city blocks.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Void Lash
components: V, S
description: You draw upon the raw, forbidden essence of the Betrayed God Nyx, lashing out with a tendril of nothingness that erases matter. Make a ranged spell attack against one creature. On a hit, the target takes 8d10 force damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have its maximum hit points reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest or receives a greater restoration spell. If a creature is reduced to 0 hit points by this spell, its body is utterly destroyed, leaving only a fine gray dust. Using this spell is considered blasphemy by the Church of the Five Skulls and may attract divine retribution if witnessed.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Ashen Legion
components: V, S, M (a handful of ash mixed with powdered obsidian worth 300 gp)
description: You call forth a legion of six skeletal soldiers from the ash of the Sulfur Wastes. Each soldier uses the statistics of a Skeletal Knight (AC 16, hp 45, multiattack with longsword and shield). They obey your orders without question, acting on your turn as a group. They are immune to fire damage and vulnerable to radiant. While you maintain concentration, they fight with unwavering discipline, and any creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of two or more of them must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened until the start of your next turn. The soldiers crumble to ash when the spell ends.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 7th-level spell slot, you summon eight skeletal knights. With an 8th-level slot, you summon ten, and with a 9th-level slot, twelve.

name: Blood Dominion
components: V, S, M (a drop of your own blood)
description: You seize control of the blood within a living creature's body. One creature you can see must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be restrained for the duration. At the start of each of its turns, the target takes 4d8 necrotic damage as its blood is forcibly manipulated. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to force the target to move up to half its speed in a direction of your choice. If the target is reduced to 0 hit points while under this effect, its blood erupts in a 15-foot-radius geyser, dealing 6d8 necrotic damage to all creatures in the area (yourself excluded). This spell is often used by House Tenebris blood-mages to humiliate and execute rivals.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the damage at the start of each turn increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Crown of the Scion
components: V, S, M (a crown of twisted obsidian worth 500 gp, which the spell consumes)
description: You channel the sovereign dignity of your bloodline, manifesting a blazing crown of crimson energy above your head. For the duration, you have resistance to all damage, and any creature that hits you with a melee attack takes 2d8 fire or necrotic damage (your choice) from the crown's backlash. Additionally, you gain advantage on Charisma checks and saving throws against being frightened or charmed. The crown sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. This spell is a sacred rite for Archon- and Scion-rank mages, signifying their right to rule.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 7th-level or higher slot, the backlash damage increases by 1d8 for every two slot levels above 5th.

name: Sovereign's Decree
components: V
description: You speak a single word of draconic authority that mortals were never meant to hear. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they are frightened for 1 minute. While frightened in this way, they are also paralyzed. At the end of each of your turns, a frightened creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. Creatures with a purity rating of 40% or higher have advantage on this save, as their blood recognizes the command. The word itself is forbidden knowledge, said to be a fragment of the Sovereigns' original language of creation.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the radius increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 5th.

name: Aether Storm
components: V, S, M (a rod of pure aether-crystal worth 200 gp)
description: You tear open the sky, calling a storm of raw aetheric energy that crackles with lightning and burning motes of force. The area within a 60-foot-radius, 100-foot-tall cylinder centered on a point you choose becomes heavily obscured and difficult terrain. At the start of each of your turns, lightning arcs down, dealing 4d10 lightning damage and 4d10 force damage to all creatures in the area (your choice if you are inside). Structures take double damage. The storm scours the land, leaving behind a faintly glowing residue that counts as difficult terrain for 24 hours. This spell is often used in aerial combat against dragon riders.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the lightning damage increases by 1d10 and the force damage by 1d10 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Flesh Weaver
components: V, S, M (a piece of preserved sinew)
description: You corrupt the flesh of up to three creatures within range. Each target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its body begins to warp uncontrollably: it gains a random mutation (choose from: extra limb that uselessly flails, skin turning to thick scales giving +2 AC but reducing speed by 10 feet, eyes melting granting blindness, or bones softening causing disadvantage on Strength checks). The DM determines the exact effect, but it should be debilitating. At the start of each of its turns, the target takes 3d8 necrotic damage as its own flesh rebels. On a success, the target takes half damage and suffers no mutation. A creature that dies while affected explodes into a spray of gore, forcing all creatures within 10 feet to succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 4d8 necrotic damage. House Tenebris uses this spell to control slaves and enemies.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 5th.

name: Burning Truth
components: V, S, M (a lit candle made from rendered heretic fat)
description: You channel the searing light of judgment, forcing truth through fire. All creatures in a 30-foot cone must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, they are wreathed in spectral flames that burn away lies: they cannot speak a deliberate falsehood for 1 minute, and they take 4d8 psychic damage if they attempt to lie. Additionally, any illusion or shapechanging effect of 5th level or lower on a creature that fails the save is dispelled, and the creature takes an additional 2d8 fire damage for each effect dispelled. On a success, a creature takes half damage and is not affected by the truth-binding. This spell is a tool of House Obsidian inquisitors used to extract confessions.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the psychic damage increases by 1d8 and the fire damage per dispelled effect increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Soul-Rend
components: V, S
description: You plunge your hand into the target's chest, tearing at the very fabric of its soul. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within reach. On a hit, the target takes 8d12 necrotic damage. If the target is a humanoid, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have its soul partially ripped out. A creature whose soul is ripped loses all class features and the ability to cast spells for 1d4 rounds, and its movement speed is halved. At the end of each of its turns, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. This spell leaves a visible scar on the spiritual plane and marks the caster as a dangerous heretic.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the damage increases by 2d12 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Tectonic Fury
components: V, S, M (a fist-sized chunk of obsidian)
description: You slam the earth, causing a localized earthquake that ravages the battlefield. The ground in a 100-foot radius centered on you becomes difficult terrain and heavily obscured by dust. At the start of each of your turns, you can choose a point within the area; the ground there erupts in a 20-foot-radius burst, forcing all creatures in that area to make a Dexterity saving throw or take 6d6 bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. Structures take double damage. Additionally, the ground is churned to a depth of 10 feet, creating a permanent change to the landscape. This spell is a signature of House Obsidian earth-mages used in sieges.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the burst damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Shadow of the Betrayed
components: V, S, M (a shard of obsidian from the Temple of Nyx)
description: You tear a hole in reality, conjuring a dark mirror of a creature you can see. The shadow copy appears within 30 feet of the target and uses the same statistics as the original, except it has only half the hit points and deals psychic damage instead of the original's damage types. The shadow obeys your commands and acts on your turn. It cannot speak or cast spells. If the original creature dies while the shadow exists, the shadow vanishes with a silent shriek, and the original's soul is trapped for 1 hour, preventing resurrection. This spell is forbidden by the Church, as it mimics the sundering of Nyx's essence.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, you can create an additional shadow copy of a different creature within range for each slot level above 5th, but each requires concentration as if they were a group.

name: Rite of the Unbroken
components: V, S, M (a vial of dragon blood worth at least 200 gp)
description: You intone an ancient hymn of the Five Skulls, blessing up to six creatures of your choice within 30 feet. For the duration, each blessed creature gains temporary hit points equal to 10 + your spellcasting ability modifier, and they have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical weapons. Additionally, when a blessed creature takes damage, it can use its reaction to deal 3d8 radiant damage to the attacker (no action required). This spell is often cast before battle by Church battle-priests leading crusades against heretics.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the temporary hit points increase by 5 for each slot level above 5th, and the radiant damage increases by 1d8.

name: Cataclysmic Amalgamation
components: V, S, M (a scale from a living Sovereign dragon, which the spell consumes)
description: You transform into a draconic avatar of destruction. For the duration, you gain the following benefits: your size becomes Large, your natural weapons deal 2d8 damage of a type matching your bloodline (fire, necrotic, lightning, etc.), you gain a flying speed of 60 feet, and you gain resistance to the damage type associated with your bloodline. Additionally, once per turn, you can exhale a 30-foot cone or 60-foot line that forces creatures in the area to make a Dexterity saving throw or take 10d6 damage of your bloodline type (half on successful save). Your gear melds into your new form. While transformed, you cannot cast other spells, but you can use your breath weapon. This spell is the pinnacle of Sovereign bloodline magic, known only to those with purity above 80%.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the breath weapon damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Tenebris's Mercy
components: V, S, M (a velvet pouch sewn from the shroud of a stillborn infant)
description: You draw upon the forbidden mercy of the Flesh-Shaper, knitting wounds with stolen life. One creature you touch regains 8d8 + 15 hit points. If the target is a construct or undead, it instead takes 8d8 + 15 necrotic damage (no save). After the healing, you can transfer any ongoing disease or poison from the target to yourself, suffering its effects for 1 minute before being cleansed. The spell leaves a faint, black scar on the recipient's skin that weeps a thin, oily fluid for 1d4 days. House Tenebris uses this as a form of cruel charity, demanding blood debts from those healed.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level or higher slot, the healing amount increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 5th, and the self-inflicted disease/poison duration increases by 1 minute.

name: Ritual of the Crimson Pyre
components: V, S, M (a shard of obsidian etched with the mark of Ignivar)
description: You channel the wrath of the Fire-Heart, causing the blood of all enemies within range to ignite from within. Each creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 8d6 fire damage and begin to burn, taking an additional 2d6 fire damage at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute, or until they or an ally uses an action to douse the flames. On a success, they take half damage and do not burn.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the initial damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Void Vortex
components: V, S, M (a fragment of a shattered Blood Crystal)
description: You tear open a miniature rift to the Void at a point you can see within range. The vortex pulls at the fabric of reality. Each creature within a 20-foot radius sphere centered on that point must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are pulled to the center, take 4d6 force damage, and are restrained for the duration. On a success, they take half damage and are not restrained. At the start of each of your turns, the vortex deals an additional 2d6 necrotic damage to all restrained creatures.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the initial force damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Inquisitor's Brand
components: V, S, M (the iron brand of an Inquisitor, heated in dragon fire)
description: You mark a creature with a searing brand that reveals its sin and weakness. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it is branded visibly for 24 hours, and you learn its alignment, its damage resistances, and one vulnerability (if any) of your choice. Additionally, for the duration, the brand glows when the creature tells a lie, giving you advantage on Insight checks against it. On a success, the creature resists the brand and is immune to this spell for 24 hours.

name: Aetheric Bloodbath
components: V, S, M (a drop of the caster's own blood, costing 1 hit die worth of health)
description: You sacrifice a portion of your own vitality to unleash a wave of aether-infused blood. All creatures of your choice within range must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 6d8 necrotic damage and are stunned until the end of your next turn as their own blood tries to tear free from their veins. On a success, they take half damage and are not stunned. For each creature that fails, you regain hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt (minimum 1 per creature).
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Flesh-Shaper's Curse
components: V, S, M (a vial of mutated blood from a Tenebris cultist)
description: You corrupt the flesh of a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its body begins to warp uncontrollably. For the duration, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks, and its speed is halved. At the start of each of your turns, the target takes 3d6 acid damage as its flesh roils and splits. If the target reaches 0 hit points while under this curse, it explodes in a spray of gore, dealing 4d6 acid damage to all creatures within 10 feet.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the initial and persistent damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Dragon's Scorn
components: V, S
description: You spew a torrent of aetherial flame mixed with the essence of a Sovereign's wrath. Each creature in a 60-foot long, 5-foot wide line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 6d10 fire damage and are marked by the dragon's scorn for 1 minute. While marked, whenever the creature deals damage to you, it takes 1d10 fire damage. On a success, they take half damage and are not marked.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Blood of the Betrayed
components: V, S, M (a tear of obsidian, symbolizing the tears of Obsidrath after the Betrayal)
description: You call upon the guilt of the Sovereigns to shield yourself. For the duration, you gain resistance to all damage from spells and magical effects. Additionally, whenever a creature within 30 feet of you casts a spell of 1st level or higher, you may use your reaction to force that creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the caster takes psychic damage equal to the spell's level (minimum 1) as the memory of the Betrayal floods its mind.

name: Soul-Searing Channel
components: V, S, M (a shard of a Blood Crystal from a Blank executed for attempting magic)
