description: You unleash a beam of pure aetheric judgment, burning through flesh and soul. Each creature in a 120-foot long, 5-foot wide line must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 7d8 radiant damage and are seared with the mark of the Church, revealing their true blood purity percentage to all who see them for 1 hour. On a success, they take half damage and are not marked. Blanks and those of Common purity (1-14%) have disadvantage on this saving throw.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Cult of the Fifth's Command
components: V, S, M (a torch lit from the perpetual flame in a Church of the Five Skulls)
description: You whisper the dogma of the Fifth Skull into the mind of a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it becomes charmed by you for the duration. While charmed, the target obeys your commands as long as those commands do not directly harm itself or its allies. The target regards you as its spiritual leader and will protect you. At the end of each of its turns, it may repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. Creatures of Archon purity or higher have advantage on the initial save, as their proud blood resents submission.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 5th.

name: Void Reclamation
components: V, S, M (a crushed Black Lotus petal and a drop of the caster's blood)
description: You tear at the boundary between life and the Void, unraveling a creature's existence in a desperate attempt to reclaim what was lost. Choose one creature within range. It must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it takes 8d10 necrotic damage, and you regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. Additionally, you can choose to resurrect one creature that has died within the last minute (including the target if it dies from this spell) as a deathless servant under your control for 1 hour. The servant has the statistics of a zombie but retains its appearance and can speak in broken phrases. On a success, the target takes half damage and is not resurrected.
higher_levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the necrotic damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 5th.

name: Sanguine Cataclysm
components: V, S, M (a vial of your own blood worth 10 HP of life force)
description: You tear open your own veins, unleashing a torrent of boiling blood that erupts from your body. All creatures of your choice within a 60-foot radius must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 12d6 necrotic damage and are blinded until the end of your next turn as blood vessels burst in their eyes. On a success, they take half damage. You lose 2d6 hit points that cannot be healed by any means for 24 hours. Aether Burn: If your Spell Safety DC fails, the damage is doubled and you are stunned for 1 minute.

name: Voidrend Lament
components: V, S
description: You whisper a song of absolute despair ripped from the void, forcing up to three creatures of your choice within range to hear it. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw or be afflicted with maddening grief. Affected creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws. On each of their turns, they must use their action to either attack the nearest creature (ally or foe) or harm themselves with a weapon or spell dealing 2d10 psychic damage. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. The spell has no effect on creatures immune to being charmed.

name: Aether-Scourge Annihilation
components: V, S, M (a shard of obsidian carved with runes of pure aether rejection)
description: You conjure a lance of anti-magic energy that tears through the fabric of reality. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature or object. On a hit, the target takes 10d10 force damage and is wracked with Aether Burn as if they had failed a Spell Safety DC of 15, causing 4d6 damage that cannot be reduced. If the target is a spellcaster, they lose all spell slots of 5th level or lower for 1 hour. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this spell is utterly disintegrated, leaving no trace.

name: Fleshweaver's Dominion
components: V, S, M (a piece of raw flesh from a sentient being)
description: You seize control of the biological matter of a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, you can reshape its body as a free action on your turn: you can cause a limb to wither (dealing 4d6 slashing damage), grow spikes from its skin (rendering it grappled and restrained), or seal its mouth (silencing it). Each turn, you can apply one of these effects. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, but each time it fails, you can choose to inflict an additional effect. If you maintain concentration for the full minute, the target's body is permanently warped (DM's discretion).

name: Cataclysm of Unmaking
components: V, S, M (the still-beating heart of a sacrificed innocent)
description: You unleash a beam of pure annihilation that destroys everything in its path. Each creature in a 300-foot long, 10-foot wide line must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 14d6 force damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. Objects and structures in the line take maximum damage. Any creature reduced to 0 hit points is erased from existence; it can only be restored by a wish spell. After casting, you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 15) or be stunned for 1 round from the backfire.

name: Curse of the Withering Soul
components: V, S, M (a withered branch from a graveyard tree and a drop of your blood)
duration: Until dispelled or removed by greater restoration
description: You lay a powerful curse upon a creature you touch. The target must make a Charisma saving throw (disadvantage if it is already cursed). On a failure, it becomes cursed: its maximum hit points are reduced by 4d10, it has disadvantage on death saving throws, and it cannot regain hit points from any source. Additionally, each dawn, the target must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 17) or lose an additional 2d10 maximum hit points. The curse persists until dispelled (requires a 9th-level dispel magic) or removed by a greater restoration spell cast at 7th level or higher. You cannot use this spell again until you complete a long rest.

name: Mind Shard Tempest
components: V, S
description: You shatter the mental defenses of all creatures in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you can see within range. Each creature in the area must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, they are overwhelmed by a tempest of psychic shards, taking 6d8 psychic damage and becoming stunned until the end of your next turn. Each turn, at the start of your turn, you can end the spell for one creature within range as a bonus action, causing it to take 4d8 psychic damage as the remaining shards burst. While this spell is active, you are blind and deaf to everything except the visions of the storm.

name: Ashen Womb of Oblivion
components: V, S, M (a handful of ashes from a cremated body)
description: You open a rift to a dimension of absolute stillness and ash. A 50-foot-radius sphere of swirling gray ash appears at a point you can see. The area within is heavily obscured and deals 2d6 cold damage and 2d6 necrotic damage to any creature that starts its turn there or enters for the first time on a turn. Creatures in the sphere are deafened and cannot speak. At the end of each of their turns, they must make a Constitution saving throw or gain one level of exhaustion. The ash clings to surfaces; any creature that leaves the sphere is covered in ash and has disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks for 1 minute.

name: Blood Price of Mastery
components: V, S, M (a ruby worth at least 1,000 gp, which the spell consumes)
description: You sacrifice a portion of your life force to temporarily elevate your magical prowess. You gain one additional 6th-level spell slot for the duration, and your Spell Safety DC is reduced by 5 for any spell you cast during this time. However, at the end of the hour, you take 10d10 necrotic damage. You cannot regain hit points from any source for the next 24 hours. If this damage kills you, your soul is destroyed and cannot be resurrected by any means short of a wish spell.

name: Soulrend Eruption
components: V, S, M (a shard of bone from a creature that died by violence)
description: You tear at the soul of a creature within range, causing a violent eruption of spiritual energy that damages both the target and nearby enemies. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it takes 10d8 psychic damage, and each creature within 15 feet of it (including you) takes 5d8 psychic damage. On a success, the target takes half damage and no secondary eruption occurs. If the target is reduced to 0 hit points, its soul is shredded and it cannot be revived except by a true resurrection or wish spell.

name: Oblivion’s Grasp
components: V, S, M (a black pearl worth at least 500 gp, which the spell consumes)
description: You summon a tendril of absolute nothingness from the void at a point you can see. The tendril has a reach of 60 feet and can make attacks against creatures you designate. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to command the tendril to attack one creature within reach: make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 8d10 necrotic damage and must make a Strength saving throw or be pulled 20 feet toward the tendril's origin. The tendril also attempts to grapple (escape DC 18). While grappled, the target takes 4d10 necrotic damage at the start of each of your turns. The tendril cannot be harmed.

name: Demonic Reprisal
casting_time: 1 reaction, which you take when you take damage from a creature within 60 feet
components: V, S
description: You channel the pain of the blow into a curse that rebounds upon your attacker. The creature that damaged you must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes the same amount of damage you just took, plus an additional 4d8 psychic damage, and it is marked for a demonic harbinger. For the next week, whenever the marked creature completes a long rest, it must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or be targeted by a summon demon spell (fiend of CR 6 or lower) that you automatically succeed on without concentration. If the creature dies while marked, its soul is consumed by the abyss.

name: Fate Severance
components: V, S, M (a golden thread worth 200 gp, which the spell consumes)
description: You touch a creature and sever its connection to the threads of fate. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw (disadvantage if you possess its true name or a piece of its hair). On a failure, the creature loses all its luck: it cannot gain advantage or inspiration, it automatically fails the first death saving throw it makes, and it has disadvantage on all saving throws until it finishes a long rest. Additionally, the next time the target would roll a natural 20 on an attack roll or saving throw, it instead rolls a natural 1. This effect ends after one week or if a remove curse spell cast at 9th level is used.

name: Aether Vortex
components: V, S, M (a crystal lens ground to a perfect circle)
description: You create a vortex of raw aether that spirals around you. All creatures within 30 feet of you must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they are pulled to the nearest unoccupied space within 10 feet of you and take 6d8 force damage. On a success, they take half damage and are not moved. While the vortex persists, any spell of 3rd level or lower cast within 60 feet of you is automatically countered (no roll needed), and the caster takes 2d6 force damage per level of the countered spell. At the start of each of your turns, you take 1d6 force damage from the strain — this cannot be reduced.

name: Coronation of the Damned
components: V, S, M (a crown of iron and bone worth at least 2,000 gp, which you must wear)
description: You undergo a dark ritual that grants you partial control over the dead. For the duration, you gain the following benefits: you can cast animate dead at will without expending a spell slot (but each use still requires material components); you have resistance to necrotic damage and immunity to the frightened condition; you can sense the presence of undead within 1 mile. However, at the end of the duration, you must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, you are afflicted with a wasting disease that reduces your maximum hit points by 4d10 each day until you die or receive a greater restoration. On a success, you are exhausted for 24 hours.

name: Unmake the Veil
components: V, S, M (a shard of obsidian etched with a dying star's rune)
description: You tear a rift between the living world and the Void. All creatures of your choice within the radius must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, their soul is partially unmade; they take 8d10 necrotic damage and are stunned until the end of your next turn. On a success, they take half damage and are not stunned. The rift lingers, reducing all healing within the area by half. Each round you maintain concentration, the rift expands by 10 feet, and any creature that starts its turn within it takes 2d10 necrotic damage. You suffer 3d6 aether burn (fire damage, ignores resistance) each time you activate this spell.

name: Soul Rendition
components: V, S, M (a locket containing a crushed heart)
description: You target one creature you can see. It must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you rip a fragment of its consciousness from its body. The target takes 10d10 psychic damage and is incapacitated for 1 minute. While incapacitated, you can issue one telepathic command that it must obey if it would not cause immediate death. On a success, it takes half damage and is not incapacitated. The soul fragment is bound to a gem you hold (value 1,000 gp) and can be used to empower a future necromantic spell. You take 4d6 aether burn (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Entropy Cascade
components: V, S, M (a handful of dust from a forgotten epoch)
description: You accelerate decay and ruin in a 40-foot-radius sphere. Nonmagical objects and structures within instantly age 1,000 years, crumbling to dust. Creatures must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they rapidly age, taking 8d10 necrotic damage and gaining one level of exhaustion. On a success, they take half damage and no exhaustion. Each subsequent round, the area expands by 20 feet, and any creature that fails its save ages further, taking an additional 4d10 necrotic damage. You suffer 4d6 aether burn each round you maintain concentration (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Grasp of the Sunken Star
components: V, S, M (a miniature black hole forged from a dying sun's core)
description: You summon a mote of collapsed reality that pulls all matter toward it. Choose a point within range. All creatures within 30 feet must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are pulled 20 feet toward the mote and take 6d10 bludgeoning damage. On a success, they are not moved and take half damage. The mote emits a gravitational pulse each round on your turn, pulling again. Creatures that end their turn within the mote's 30-foot radius are restrained until the start of your next turn. The mote leaves a scar in reality; the area becomes difficult terrain permanently. You take 4d6 aether burn (fire damage, ignores resistance) when you cast and each round you sustain it.

name: Weave of Silence
components: V, S, M (a thread from a burial shroud)
description: You create a zone of absolute silence and sensory deprivation. Within the area, all sound is nullified; creatures are deafened and cannot speak or cast spells with verbal components. Additionally, all light dims to near-darkness, granting disadvantage on Perception checks relying on sight. Creatures inside must make a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of their turns or become frightened of the emptiness, possibly suffering psychic damage equal to their level. The zone moves with you. You are immune to these effects but take 3d6 aether burn upon casting (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Bonefather's Bargain
components: V, S, M (a skeletal hand and a drop of your blood)
description: You touch a dead creature that died within the last 7 days. Its soul is dragged back from the abyss, but at a terrible cost. The target returns to life with 1 hit point, but its alignment shifts to neutral evil, and it is under your control for 24 hours. At the end of that period, it crumbles to dust. While controlled, the creature has advantage on attack rolls and saving throws, but it must feed on the lifeforce of living creatures each day or take 4d10 necrotic damage that cannot be healed. You suffer 5d6 aether burn (fire damage, ignores resistance) and gain one level of exhaustion.

name: Reality's Fracture
components: V, S, M (a cracked mirror that reflects only darkness)
description: You shatter the fabric of causality in a 30-foot radius. For the duration, any creature within the area (including you) must roll a d20 twice for every attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, and take the lower result. Additionally, any spell cast within the area triggers wild magic (roll on a custom grimdark wild magic table). Creatures that start their turn in the area take 2d10 psychic damage from glimpsing fractured timelines. You are not immune to the disadvantage but you do not take the psychic damage. You suffer 4d6 aether burn upon casting (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Withering Chorus
components: V, S, M (a dead songbird preserved in amber)
description: You unleash a discordant wave of entropic sound. All creatures in a 60-foot cone must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, their bodies begin to desiccate and wither; they take 12d8 necrotic damage and their hit point maximum is reduced by the same amount until they finish a long rest. On a success, they take half damage and no maximum reduction. The sound leaves an agonizing ringing; affected creatures are deafened for 1 minute. You take 5d6 aether burn (fire damage, ignores resistance) and are deafened for 1 round.

name: Pact of the Unborn
components: V, S, M (a stillborn's heart wrapped in black silk)
description: You bargain with unborn souls for protection. You gain resistance to all damage for the duration. Whenever you are hit by an attack or fail a saving throw, the unborn souls scream, and you must make a Charisma saving throw (DC 16) or take 3d10 psychic damage (which cannot be resisted). Additionally, when you are reduced to 0 hit points while this spell is active, you can choose to fall to 1 hit point instead, but the spell ends and you gain 2 levels of exhaustion. The souls' screams are audible to all within 30 feet. You suffer 4d6 aether burn when casting (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Crown of Rot
components: V, S, M (a circlet of bone worn by a leper king)
description: You don a crown of decay that grants you dominion over rot and disease. While wearing it, you are immune to poison and disease, and your melee weapon attacks deal an extra 2d6 necrotic damage. As an action, you can force all creatures within 30 feet to make a Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute. Additionally, you can curse one creature within 60 feet with a wasting disease that deals 4d6 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute (no save, but ends on a successful DC 16 Constitution save at end of its turns). The crown visibly decays your flesh; you take 3d6 aether burn upon casting and each hour you maintain it (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Stare into the Abyss
components: V, S, M (a polished obsidian orb that reflects nothing)
description: You open your mind fully to the Void, gaining insights beyond mortal comprehension. For the duration, you have truesight out to 120 feet, can see invisible creatures, and can read surface thoughts of any creature within 60 feet (Wisdom save DC 17 to resist). However, the Abyss gazes back: at the end of each of your turns, you must make a Charisma saving throw DC 15 or take 3d10 psychic damage and be frightened until the start of your next turn. If you fail by 5 or more, you are stunned for 1 round. You also suffer 4d6 aether burn when you cast (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Bloodfall
components: V, S, M (a vial of blood from a murdered innocent)
description: You open a portal to a realm of endless blood, causing a torrent of crimson liquid to cascade down upon a 30-foot radius area. Creatures in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone and take 8d6 bludgeoning damage from the force of the deluge. On a success, they take half damage and are not knocked prone. The blood is slippery; the area becomes difficult terrain, and creatures that end their turn there must make a Dexterity save or fall prone. The blood is also cursed: any creature that touches it must succeed on a Constitution save or be poisoned for 1 minute. You take 4d6 aether burn when casting (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Soul Anchor
components: V, S, M (a chain forged from a condemned man's soul)
description: You bind a willing creature's soul to the material plane, preventing death. For the duration, if the target would drop to 0 hit points, it instead drops to 1 hit point and the spell ends. Additionally, it has advantage on death saving throws. However, the binding is painful: the target takes 2d6 psychic damage each time it is damaged by a creature (once per round). When the spell ends, the target must make a Constitution save DC 16 or gain one level of exhaustion. You take 3d6 aether burn upon casting (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Gift of Unmaking
components: V, S, M (a finger bone from a stillborn child)
description: You point at a creature and whisper a word of annihilation. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its body begins to unravel; it takes 10d10 necrotic damage and is reduced to 0 hit points if this damage reduces it to below half its maximum. Even on a success, it takes 5d10 necrotic damage and its hit point maximum is reduced by that amount until it finishes a long rest. A creature killed by this spell is erased from existence, leaving no body and requiring True Resurrection or Wish to return. You suffer 6d6 aether burn (fire damage, ignores resistance) and gain one level of exhaustion.

name: Echo of the First Sin
components: V, S, M (a forbidden text written in blood on human skin)
description: You force a creature to relive the primordial crime that birthed mortality. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it is overwhelmed by guilt and despair, taking 12d8 psychic damage and becoming incapacitated for 1 minute. On a success, it takes half damage and is not incapacitated. While incapacitated, it is catatonic, unable to move or act. At the end of each of its turns, it can repeat the save, ending the effect on a success. If the target is reduced to 0 hit points by this spell, its soul is shattered and cannot be resurrected except by divine intervention. You take 5d6 aether burn (fire damage, ignores resistance).

name: Monarch's Desanguination
components: V, S, M (a drop of the caster's own blood)
description: You tear the lifeblood from every creature in a 60-foot radius. Each non-construct, non-undead creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, they take 12d8 necrotic damage and you gain temporary hit points equal to half the damage dealt. On success, they take half damage and you gain no temporary hit points. The ground is slick with blood, and the air fills with a coppery mist. This spell leaves the caster's veins aching, but the stolen essence fortifies them beyond mortal limits.

name: Sovereign's Aether Burst
components: V, S, M (a core of pure aether crystal)
description: You unleash a cataclysmic pulse of raw aether. All creatures in a 100-foot radius must make a Dexterity saving throw. On failure, they take 20d6 radiant damage and are blinded for 1 minute. On success, half damage and no blindness. Undead and fiends have disadvantage on the save. The blast leaves a scorched, glassy crater and the caster momentarily glows with internal light. Despite the god-tier power, the strain may ignite your own veins—roll Spell Safety.

name: Gravebind Singularity
components: V, S, M (a black hole sliver from a fallen star)
description: You tear open a micro-singularity at a point you can see. All creatures within a 40-foot radius must make a Strength saving throw or be pulled toward the center, taking 8d10 force damage and being restrained. Each turn the singularity persists, it pulls creatures 20 feet inward, dealing 4d10 force damage at the end of their turn if they are restrained. The gravitational distortion rips flesh and shatters bone. The caster must maintain unwavering will, or the singularity collapses prematurely.

name: Blood Sovereign's Dominion
components: V, S, M (the caster's blood drawn into a chalice)
description: You seize control of the blood within all creatures of your choice in a 60-foot cone. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On failure, you gain telepathic dominance over them as per the Dominate Monster spell, but with unlimited range as long as you maintain concentration. Controlled creatures have veins visibly pulsing in sync with your heartbeat. You can issue commands as a bonus action. If a creature dies under your control, its blood erupts in a 10-foot burst dealing 6d6 necrotic damage to nearby enemies.

name: Cataclysmic Vein Rupture
components: V, S, M (a dried artery of a powerful vampire)
description: You force the blood within up to six creatures you can see to explosively rupture. Each target must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, they take 15d10 necrotic damage and begin bleeding profusely, losing 2d10 hit points at the start of each of their turns until a healer stops the bleeding. On success, half damage and no ongoing bleed. The sheer violence of this magic paints the area in gore and may inflict aether burn on the caster if their blood purity wavers.

name: Ancestral Blood Rite
components: V, S, M (blood from at least ten ancient warriors, each worth 50+ gp)
description: You perform a forbidden rite using the blood of ancient warriors to call forth the spirits of Slaughter-Kings and Plague-Queens. Upon completion, you summon up to three Blood Wraiths (use Nightwalker stats, but deal necrotic damage and are immune to piercing). They obey your mental commands for 24 hours. Additionally, a Blood Shrine is created at the site, oozing aether that boosts your spell safety DC by 5 for all necromancy spells cast within 100 feet for the next year. The shrine corrupts the land, and the spirits whisper secrets of eternal damnation.

name: Null-Core Detonation
components: V, S, M (a shattered focus from a dead archmage)
description: You detonate a core of anti-magic that tears through all spells and aether. All magical effects within 80 feet are dispelled (as per Dispel Magic at 8th level). Each creature in the area that can cast spells must make a Charisma saving throw. On failure, they take 10d8 force damage and lose all spell slots for 1 minute. On success, half damage and lose one spell slot. A caster who fails also gains 3 levels of aether burn (automatic damage per spell level). The shockwave leaves a dead zone where magic sputters for 1d4 rounds.

name: Eternal Sanguination
components: V, S, M (a vial of pure aether and a drop of the target's blood)
description: You transmute the blood of one creature into volatile aether. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, it takes 10d10 necrotic damage immediately and at the start of each of its turns for the duration, and its blood becomes a ticking bomb. If it takes fire or force damage while the spell is active, it explodes in a 20-foot radius, dealing 20d10 force damage to all creatures (including itself). The explosion ends the spell. On successful save, the target takes half damage and the blood remains normal. This magic is so unstable that even the caster may suffer backlash if the target dies during transmutation.

name: Aetherial Bloodstorm
components: V, S, M (blood from a sacrificed archmage)
description: You summon a storm of blood and raw aether in a colossal cylinder. The area becomes heavily obscured. All creatures in the storm must make a Constitution saving throw at the start of their turns. On failure, they take 6d10 necrotic damage and are blinded until the end of their next turn. On success, half damage. Additionally, any spellcaster in the storm must make a concentration check to cast spells (DC 15 + spell level), or the spell fails and they take aether burn equal to the level. The storm feeds on blood: if a creature dies within it, the storm strengthens, increasing the damage by 1d10 for the rest of its duration.

name: God-Heart Awakening
components: V, S, M (the caster's own heart's blood, drawn with a silver needle)
description: You forcibly awaken a portion of your Sovereign blood, temporarily raising your effective blood purity to 100%. For the duration, you automatically pass all Spell Safety checks, your spells deal maximum damage, and you can cast one additional 9th-level spell (if you have a slot). However, the strain is immense: at the end of the minute, you take 20d10 force damage that cannot be reduced, and your actual blood purity drops by 10% permanently (this can reduce you below the threshold to cast high-level spells until restored via rare rituals). Your veins glow with god-light, and those nearby sense your terrifying ascendance.

name: Phantom Blood Lance
components: V, S, M (a lance of iron dipped in the caster's blood)
description: You conjure a massive lance of congealed, crystalline blood that streaks toward a target. Make a ranged spell attack. On hit, the target takes 16d10 piercing damage plus 8d10 necrotic damage, and its blood begins to crystallize. The target must make a Constitution saving throw or have its speed reduced to 0 and be unable to take reactions until the end of its next turn. The lance then explodes, dealing 6d10 force damage to all creatures within 15 feet of the target. The shards remain as difficult terrain. The caster's own blood pressure spikes dangerously; if the attack misses, the lance shatters in the caster's hands, dealing 4d10 necrotic damage to the caster.

name: Soul-Cage Hemomancy
components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 5,000 gp, consumed, and the caster's own blood over the course of the hour)
description: You trap the soul of a recently deceased creature (died within 24 hours) within a blood-red crystal. The soul is aware and can communicate telepathically with you. While the crystal exists, you can use an action to command the soul to perform one task: answer a question truthfully (as per Commune, no limit on questions), possess a corpse nearby (as per Magic Jar), or unleash a burst of necrotic energy (10d10 in a 30-foot cone). The crystal has AC 15, 30 hit points, and requires a successful DC 20 Strength check to break. If smashed, the soul is released and cannot be recaptured. This spell is often used to torture and extract secrets from powerful enemies before their final death.

name: Soul-Sunder Cataclysm
components: V, S, M (a crystalline heart of a willing archon, worth 50,000 gp)
description: You shatter the boundary between life and death in a vast region. Every creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30). On a failure, their soul is ripped from their body and consumed by the aether, leaving a husk that rises as a thrall under your control. On a success, they take 20d10 necrotic damage and gain one level of exhaustion. The land itself becomes cursed; no resurrection magic works within the radius for 1d100 years.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20 (ignoring purity bonus due to soul-crushing backlash). On failure, caster takes 9d6 aether burn and their blood purity permanently drops by 1d10 percentage points.
purity_gate: Requires 70% purity or higher to attempt. If purity is below 95%, the caster must also succeed on a DC 25 Wisdom saving throw or be driven mad (indefinite madness).
special: If cast by a Sovereign (Awakened), the purity cost is negated, but the DM rolls on a world-shaking event table.

name: Graveheart Singularity
components: V, S, M (a black hole shard from a dead star, worth 100,000 gp)
description: You create a point of absolute gravitational collapse at the target location. All creatures and objects within a 500-foot radius must make a Strength saving throw (DC 30) or be pulled to the center, taking 30d10 bludgeoning damage and being restrained. The singularity persists; each round, the radius expands by 100 feet. Non-magical structures are crushed. At the end of the duration, the singularity implodes, dealing 50d10 force damage to everything within 1 mile and creating a permanent crater 2 miles wide.
safety_check: Constitution DC 18. On failure, caster takes 9d6 aether burn and is lifted 100 feet into the air by gravitational rebounding, taking 10d10 falling damage.
purity_gate: Requires 80% purity. The caster must also have a Strength score of at least 16 to withstand the pull.
special: If the caster loses concentration, the singularity detonates prematurely.

name: Endless Night's Ascendancy
components: V, S, M (a vial of shadow from the Plane of Darkness, absorbed into the caster's bloodstream)
description: You become a being of pure shadow and entropy. For the duration, you gain immunity to all damage except radiant, and you can pass through any solid object. Any creature that starts its turn within 30 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30) or take 10d10 necrotic damage and have its maximum hit points reduced by the same amount. You can also, as an action, extinguish all light sources within 1 mile (magical light requires a DC 25 dispel check). The sky above becomes pitch black, blocking the sun and stars. Crops wither, and the temperature drops to freezing.
safety_check: Constitution DC 17. On failure, you become permanently incorporeal (as a ghost) and lose your body. Only a Wish or Deific intervention can restore you.
purity_gate: Requires 85% purity. If the caster's purity is below 90%, they must also succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC 20) or become an evil shadow creature under the DM's control.
special: While in this form, the caster cannot cast other spells.

name: Tear the Weave of Fate
components: V, S, M (a golden thread woven from the hair of a sphinx, worth 200,000 gp)
description: You reach into the fabric of causality and rip out a single event. Choose one event that occurred within the last 1,000 years (no larger than a continent-wide war or the death of a godlike being). That event is undone. All creatures that died in that event are resurrected (if their souls are still available). All consequences are reversed, but the timeline may splinter. The caster experiences immediate memory of both timelines and must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 25) or suffer indefinite madness.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20, disadvantage due to the strain of rewriting reality. On failure, the caster's body disintegrates into temporal dust. Their soul is lost in the timestream.
purity_gate: Requires 95% purity (only Sovereigns can attempt). Even then, the spell may provoke the wrath of time deities.
special: This spell cannot be undone. The DM determines side effects.

name: Plague of Unmaking
components: V, S, M (the corpse of a creature that died of a magical plague, preserved in aetherite jar)
description: You unleash a magical plague that spreads through the aether itself. Every creature within 10 miles must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30) or contract the plague. Victims take 5d10 necrotic damage per day, cannot regain hit points, and each day the saving throw DC increases by 1. The plague is airborne and magical; it can cross planar boundaries. If a creature dies from the plague, it rises as a plague zombie that spreads the disease. There is no known cure except for a Greater Restoration cast at 9th level or the direct intervention of a deity.
safety_check: Constitution DC 19. On failure, the caster contracts the plague themselves (no save). They become the first patient zero and cannot be cured unless they succeed on a DC 30 Constitution check after 24 hours.
purity_gate: Requires 75% purity. If the caster's purity is below 90%, the plague may also affect allies without discrimination.
special: The plague cannot be dispelled; it is a supernatural curse.

name: Cataclysmic Geomancy
components: V, S, M (a tectonic plate shard from the Elemental Plane of Earth, held by the caster)
description: You cause massive geological upheaval. Choose a point on the ground: a 10-mile radius area becomes a volcano erupting magma (20d10 fire damage per round to everything inside, plus lava flows). Alternatively, you can cause an earthquake that splits the continent (creates a rift 1 mile wide and 10 miles long). Structures in a 100-mile radius collapse. Tsunamis are triggered if near water. The changes are permanent.
safety_check: Constitution DC 18. On failure, the caster is buried under collapsing earth (10d10 bludgeoning damage) and is restrained until freed.
purity_gate: Requires 80% purity. The caster must have a minimum Constitution of 18.
special: Cannot be cast indoors or underground without causing immediate collapse on caster.

name: Mind of the Dead God
components: V, S, M (the skull of a dead god, which crumbles to dust after casting)
description: You channel the consciousness of a deceased deity. For the duration, you can read the thoughts of any creature within 1 mile, and you can issue a single command that all creatures within range must obey (no save, unless they are immune to mind-affecting effects). If you choose, you can also absorb the god's memories—this gives you advantage on all Intelligence checks but also risks insanity (Wisdom save DC 25 or suffer permanent madness). After the spell ends, you are stunned for 1 round and take 10d10 psychic damage.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20. On failure, the god's personality overrides yours; you become an NPC under the DM's control (possibly a new villain).
purity_gate: Requires 90% purity. Even then, the caster's alignment shifts permanently to the god's alignment (usually evil).
special: Only one such skull exists in the world per god. Using this spell may attract the attention of living gods.

name: Soulforged Desolation
components: V, S, M (a soul jar containing 1,000 captured souls, which are released and consumed)
description: You open a rift to the Negative Energy Plane, allowing a tide of anti-life to flood the area. All non-undead creatures in the radius must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30). On a failure, they take 40d10 necrotic damage and are reduced to 0 hit points. On a success, they take half damage and their maximum hit points are reduced by the damage taken. The area becomes a dead magic zone for 1d100 years; no magic functions there (including resurrection). The ground becomes lifeless and barren.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20. On failure, the caster's soul is sucked into the Negative Energy Plane; their body becomes a wraith under DM control.
purity_gate: Requires 95% purity OR the caster must be undead (lich/vampire). The spell consumes 1,000 souls from the jar.
special: Casting this spell is considered an atrocity; good-aligned deities may smite the caster.

name: Word of Unmaking
components: V (a guttural utterance that can be heard for 10 miles)
description: You speak a syllable that negates magic itself. All spells and magical effects of 8th level or lower in the cone are dispelled automatically. Creatures concentrating on spells must make a Constitution save (DC 30) or lose the spell. Additionally, any creature in the cone that is magically created (like summoned creatures, golems, etc.) must make a Charisma saving throw (DC 30) or be permanently destroyed. The sound itself deals 10d10 thunder damage to all creatures in the cone (no save). After casting, you cannot speak for 1 hour (magically silenced).
safety_check: Constitution DC 18. On failure, you are permanently deafened and take 10d10 psychic damage from the backlash of the word.
purity_gate: Requires 85% purity. The caster must know at least one language of primordial power (Draconic, Celestial, Infernal, etc.).
special: This word can also harm artifacts; roll a d20. On a 1, an artifact in range is destroyed.

name: Raynok's Final Storm
components: V, S, M (a lightning-rod from a storm giant's castle, charged by a god's wrath)
description: You call down a storm that covers a 10-mile radius. Within that area, the skies are pitch black, and every round, a lightning bolt strikes a random creature (10d10 lightning damage, Dex save DC 30 for half). Additionally, tornadoes form (5d10 bludgeoning damage each round to anything in their path). The storm also causes torrential rain that floods low-lying areas within 10 miles. The storm cannot be dispelled by any means less than a Wish or divine intervention. After the spell ends, the area is devastated.
safety_check: Constitution DC 17. On failure, the caster is struck by a bolt of lightning (20d10 lightning damage, no save) and the storm goes rogue (DM controls it).
purity_gate: Requires 80% purity. The caster must have a natural connection to storms (e.g., Storm Sorcery origin, or control weather spell known).
special: If the caster loses concentration, the storm dissipates over 1d4 hours, but the damage is done.

name: Pact of the Hollow Crown
components: V, S, M (a crown forged from the iron of a thousand hanged kings, worth 500,000 gp)
description: You create a permanent enchantment on a ruler or monarch (willing or unconscious) that binds their soul to a crown. The crown becomes an artifact that controls the ruler's mind; the ruler loses all free will and becomes a puppet. Additionally, all creatures within 1 mile that are loyal to the ruler (officials, guards, etc.) must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 30) or become enslaved to the crown as well. The crown can be used to issue commands telepathically across any distance. Destroying the crown kills the ruler.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20. On failure, the caster's soul is trapped inside the crown instead; they become the puppet.
purity_gate: Requires 90% purity. The caster must also sacrifice a humanoid of noble birth (their choice) or the spell fails and backfires.
special: This spell is considered a high crime against the natural order. Paladins and clerics may sense the disturbance.

name: Oblivion's Embrace
components: V, S, M (a shard from a destroyed plane, which disintegrates after casting)
description: You channel raw oblivion, the stuff of non-existence. Every creature in the cone must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 30). On a failure, they are erased from existence—no body, no soul, no afterlife. They cannot be resurrected by any means short of a divine act of creation. On a success, they take 30d10 force damage and are permanently blinded and deafened. The caster also takes 10d10 force damage as backlash. The ground in the cone becomes a grey, lifeless zone where nothing grows for 1,000 years.
safety_check: Constitution DC 20. On failure, the caster is also erased from existence. No save.
purity_gate: Requires 95% purity (only Sovereigns). If the caster is not fully awakened, they automatically fail the safety check.
special: This spell cannot be counterspelled. It does not generate aether burn; it consumes the caster's own essence instead.
